title: Craspedostoma penicillatum
taxonomy: Craspedostoma penicillatum belongs to the phylum Mollusca, specifically within the class Gastropoda. It is further classified under the order Neotremata, which includes freshwater snails. The family in which Craspedostoma penicillatum is classified is Pachychilidae. This family is a group of freshwater snails found primarily in Southeast Asia. They are known for their distinctive shell morphology and are often found in fast-moving streams and rivers.
uses: Craspedostoma penicillatum is a species of freshwater snail with reported medicinal uses. Its extracts have been found to possess antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. The snail's shell and other body parts are used in traditional medicine in some Southeast Asian countries to treat various health conditions. The exact mechanisms and effects of Craspedostoma penicillatum's medicinal uses require further research. More studies are needed to confirm its potential therapeutic applications and to standardize its use in medicine.
